trades.sentiment_summary: Aptiv announced a significant deal with a leading Indian commercial vehicle OEM to use its Gen 6 ADAS platform, driving a 7.4% stock price increase.[2] The company is also showcasing its next-generation AI-powered Gen 8 radar and LINC software stack at CES 2026, reinforcing its strategic pivot toward software-led safety and ADAS solutions.[2][6]

Clearwater Analytics (CWAN) has agreed to be acquired for $8.4 billion by Permira and Warburg Pincus, with participation from Temasek and support from Francisco Partners, offering shareholders $24.55 per share—a 47% premium over the undisturbed price and 13% over recent close[1][3][5]. The deal includes a go-shop period until January 23, 2026, to seek better offers, and is expected to close in H1 2026, with the company delisting from NYSE[1][3]. Recent insider sales by executives like Subi Sethi and Scott Stanley Erickson occurred on December 31, 2025, amid strong revenue growth forecasts[2][4][7].

The European Commission granted unconditional antitrust approval for Omnicom's acquisition of IPG, marking the final regulatory clearance needed to complete the transaction.[2] Bank of America downgraded Omnicom to Underperform on January 5, 2026, citing growth risks from the $13B IPG deal that outweigh anticipated benefits, leading to lowered price targets and earnings forecasts.[6][7][8] No other major IPG-specific news appears in the last 24 hours.

KKR announced a $1.5 billion equity commitment to Global Technical Realty (GTR), its European data center platform, with Oak Hill Capital adding $400 million, totaling nearly $2 billion to expand AI-driven infrastructure across Europe[1][4][6]. Reports also emerged of KKR acquiring Arctos Partners, signaling active deal-making[2]. KKR stock traded positively in after-hours at $136.70, up from the previous close of $135.82[3][5].
